req.title: Schedule performance review — variant 2
req.statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.
preconditions: role:manager

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a scheduling rule (>=7 days in advance), an error pathway (422 for <7 days), and an idempotency constraint. I applied EP to cover a clearly valid date well inside the allowed window; BVA to probe the exact boundary at now+7d (inclusive lower bound); NEG to confirm rejection of a date 6 days out (one day inside the forbidden zone); STC to model the full state transition from 'unscheduled' through 'scheduled' to a confirmed idempotent retry; and DT to enumerate the decision combinations around the 7-day rule with notification side-effects. Together these five tests give full coverage of the stated acceptance criteria and error pathways.

tests.name: test_schedule_review_valid_date_accepted
preconditions: Authenticated manager session exists
Reviewee user exists and is active
Target date is now + 14 days (well within valid partition)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Notification flag or event is present in response
trace: EP: date in valid partition (>= now+7d)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_valid_date_accepted(manager_session, reviewee_id, api_base_url):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=14)).isoformat()
    payload = {"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at}
    resp = manager_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response must contain review id"
    assert body.get("status") == "scheduled"
    assert body.get("notifications_sent") is True

tests.name: test_schedule_review_past_date_rejected_422
preconditions: Authenticated manager session exists
Reviewee user exists and is active
Target date is now - 1 day (clearly in the forbidden partition)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error message referencing 7-day rule
trace: R-WEB-093: error pathway — 422 when scheduled_at < now+7d
NEG: date in invalid partition (past date)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_past_date_rejected_422(manager_session, reviewee_id, api_base_url):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at}
    resp = manager_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_msg = str(body).lower()
    assert "7" in error_msg or "advance" in error_msg or "scheduled_at" in error_msg

tests.name: test_schedule_review_exactly_7_days_boundary_accepted
preconditions: Authenticated manager session exists
Reviewee user exists and is active
Target date is exactly now + 7 days (lower valid boundary)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Boundary value is treated as inclusive lower bound
trace: R-WEB-093: 'at least 7 days in advance' — boundary inclusive
BVA: exactly now+7d is the minimum valid value
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_exactly_7_days_boundary_accepted(manager_session, reviewee_id, api_base_url):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=7, seconds=30)).isoformat()
    payload = {"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at}
    resp = manager_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 at 7-day boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "scheduled"

tests.name: test_schedule_review_6_days_boundary_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated manager session exists
Reviewee user exists and is active
Target date is now + 6 days (one day below the valid boundary)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body references the 7-day advance-notice rule
trace: R-WEB-093: sub-7-day dates shall be rejected
BVA: now+6d is the first value on the invalid side of the boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_6_days_boundary_rejected(manager_session, reviewee_id, api_base_url):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=6)).isoformat()
    payload = {"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at}
    resp = manager_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422 for 6-day date, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body, "Error response body must not be empty"

tests.name: test_schedule_review_idempotent_on_retry
preconditions: Authenticated manager session exists
Reviewee user exists and is active
No existing review between this manager and reviewee for the target date
Target date is now + 14 days
actions: POST /performance-reviews/schedule with scheduled_at = now + 14 days (first call)
Capture review id from first response
POST identical payload a second time (retry)
expected: Both calls return non-5xx status codes
Review id returned on retry matches original id OR a 200/201 is returned without duplicate record
Exactly one review record exists after both calls
trace: R-WEB-093: action must be idempotent under retry
STC: transition unscheduled -> scheduled -> scheduled (retry leaves state unchanged)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_idempotent_on_retry(manager_session, reviewee_id, api_base_url, list_reviews_for_pair):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=14)).isoformat()
    payload = {"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at}
    resp1 = manager_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert resp1.status_code in (200, 201), f"First call failed: {resp1.status_code}"
    review_id_1 = resp1.json().get("id")
    resp2 = manager_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert resp2.status_code in (200, 201), f"Retry call failed: {resp2.status_code}"
    review_id_2 = resp2.json().get("id")
    assert review_id_1 == review_id_2, "Idempotent retry must return same review id"
    records = list_reviews_for_pair(manager_session, reviewee_id, scheduled_at, api_base_url)
    assert len(records) == 1, f"Expected 1 review record, found {len(records)}"
    assert records[0].get("status") == "scheduled"

tests.name: test_schedule_review_decision_table_role_and_date_combinations
preconditions: Both a manager session and a non-manager (employee) session are available
Reviewee user exists and is active
Two target dates prepared: valid (now+14d) and invalid (now+3d)
actions: Attempt scheduling with non-manager role and valid date — expect 403
Attempt scheduling with manager role and invalid date — expect 422
Attempt scheduling with manager role and valid date — expect 201
trace: R-WEB-093: error pathway 422 for sub-7-day date
DT: all combinations of {role: manager|non-manager} x {date: valid|invalid}
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_decision_table_role_and_date_combinations(
    manager_session, employee_session, reviewee_id, api_base_url
):
    valid_date = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=14)).isoformat()
    invalid_date = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=3)).isoformat()
    url = f"{api_base_url}/performance-reviews/schedule"

    # Row 1: non-manager + valid date -> 403
    r1 = employee_session.post(url, json={"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": valid_date})
    assert r1.status_code == 403, f"Non-manager should be forbidden; got {r1.status_code}"

    # Row 2: manager + invalid date -> 422
    r2 = manager_session.post(url, json={"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": invalid_date})
    assert r2.status_code == 422, f"Manager + sub-7d date should be 422; got {r2.status_code}"

    # Row 3: manager + valid date -> 201
    r3 = manager_session.post(url, json={"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": valid_date})
    assert r3.status_code == 201, f"Manager + valid date should be 201; got {r3.status_code}"
